Mixed martial arts events
2006 in Shooto
Information
First dateFebruary 17, 2006
Last dateDecember 2, 2006
Events
Total events26
Fights
Total fights186
Title fights6
Chronology
2005 in Shooto 2006 in Shooto 2007 in Shooto

The year 2006 is the 18th year in the history of Shooto, a mixed martial arts promotion based in Japan. In 2006 Shooto held 26 events beginning with, Shooto: The Victory of the Truth.

Title fights

[edit]
Title fights in 2006
Weight class Method Round Time Event Notes
Lightweight Tatsuya Kawajiri (c) def. Joachim Hansen DQ (Kick to Groin) 1 0:08 Shooto: The Victory of the Truth [a]
Welterweight Shinya Aoki def. Akira Kikuchi (c) Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00 Shooto: The Victory of the Truth [b]
Flyweight Mamoru Yamaguchi (c) vs. Shinichi Kojima Draw 3 5:00 Shooto: 3/24 in Korakuen Hall [c]
Featherweight Takeshi Inoue def. Antonio Carvalho TKO (Punches) 1 3:06 Shooto: The Devilock [d]
Bantamweight Akitoshi Hokazono def. Kenji Osawa Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00 Shooto 2006: 7/21 in Korakuen Hall [e]
Flyweight Shinichi Kojima def. Mamoru Yamaguchi (c) Technical Submission (Rear-Naked Choke) 1 1:38 Shooto: Champion Carnival [f]
  1. ^ For the Lightweight Championship.
  2. ^ For the Welterweight Championship.
  3. ^ For the Flyweight Championship.
  4. ^ For the Featherweight Championship.
  5. ^ For the Bantamweight Championship.
  6. ^ For the Flyweight Championship.

Shooto: The Victory of the Truth

[edit]

Shooto: The Victory of the Truth[1] was an event held on February 17, 2006 at Yoyogi National Gymnasium in Tokyo, Japan.

Results

[edit]
Main Card
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Lightweight Tatsuya Kawajiri (c) def. Joachim Hansen DQ (Kick to Groin) 1 0:08 [a]
Welterweight Shinya Aoki def. Akira Kikuchi (c) Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00 [b]

Tenkei Oda def. Hiroyuki Abe Decision (Unanimous) 3 5:00

Kenji Osawa def. Naoya Uematsu Decision (Majority) 3 5:00
Megumi Fujii def. Misaki Takimoto Technical Submission (Armbar) 2 4:36
  1. ^ For the Lightweight Championship.
  2. ^ For the Welterweight Championship.
